title: rubus inflexatus
intro: Rubus inflexatus, also known as the Japanese black raspberry or Japanese wild raspberry, is a species of bramble that is native to East Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to its high levels of anthocyanins and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rubus inflexatus include potential anti-cancer, anti-aging, and cardiovascular-protective effects, making it a popular ingredient in herbal supplements and teas. Rubus inflexatus can be prepared as a tea, powder, or extract, and is often used to support immune function, reduce inflammation, and promote overall well-being.
